title: Account registration, login, and bearer-token auth
body:
## Goal
Add POST /auth/register and POST /auth/login with salted password hashing and HMAC-signed bearer tokens, plus the auth verifier that the foundation router applies to protected routes to resolve the current user.

## Changes
- {"path":"src/auth.js","assumption":"creates"}
- {"path":"src/routes/auth.js","assumption":"creates"}
- {"path":"tests/auth.test.js","assumption":"creates"}

## Acceptance
- [ ] POST /auth/register with {"username","password"} creates a user and responds with the new user's id and username; the stored password is a salted hash, never plaintext.
- [ ] POST /auth/register with a duplicate username or a missing/blank username or password is rejected with a sensible 4xx JSON error and does not crash the server.
- [ ] POST /auth/login with valid credentials responds with {"token"} (an HMAC-signed bearer token); login with wrong credentials responds with 401.
- [ ] The auth verifier, given a request with no Authorization header or an invalid/tampered/expired bearer token, rejects it with a 401 and resolves no user — asserted directly against the verifier in tests/auth.test.js.
- [ ] The auth verifier, given a request carrying a bearer token minted by /auth/login, resolves the correct current user — asserted directly against the verifier, so the contract is provable before any protected resource route exists.
- [ ] The verifier is installed into the foundation router's auth-injection point so that routes registered as protected are gated by it.

## Verify
- node --test tests/auth.test.js (contract)
- npm run typecheck (validate)

> 🏷️ Authored with Mandrel v0.0.0 · 2026-07-12

<!-- meta: {"reason_to_exist":"Authentication is a distinct security boundary (password hashing + token signing) that every protected endpoint depends on (b: isolates the auth/crypto boundary into its own reviewable unit).","mandrel_version":"0.0.0","authored_at":"2026-07-12"} -->
